description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a wide, overhanging hip roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The exterior walls are a light beige color, accented by horizontal bands and structural supports in a darker tan or ochre shade. Each story features a row of evenly spaced, arched windows with green-tinted glass, giving the house a symmetrical and orderly appearance. The upper and lower floors are separated by prominent wraparound balconies or verandas, supported by angular posts at each corner. The house has an open, airy feel, with visible interior space through the central windows and balcony openings. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and colonial architectural influences, with a focus on symmetry and covered outdoor spaces.
description: A two-story house with a wide, tiled hip roof, symmetrical arched windows, and wraparound balconies supported by angular posts.
description_view_0: This view shows the house from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the upper balcony, roof tiles, and the symmetrical arrangement of arched windows.
description_view_1: This view presents the house head-on, emphasizing the central row of windows and the open balcony area, with clear visibility of the supporting posts.
description_view_2: This view is similar to view 0 but from the opposite side, showing the same symmetrical window and balcony arrangement, with a focus on the lower story.
description_view_3: This view displays the house from the rear or another side, again highlighting the wraparound balconies and the consistent window pattern.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with a covered balcony above, supported by angular posts and flanked by arched windows.
